blind_restrictions: No WebSearch/WebFetch. All 9 attack vectors applied using parametric knowledge only.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Nucleates Mechanosensitive Super-Enhancers via YAP-Dependent BRD4/MED1 Phase Condensate Assembly
key_issues: Zanconato et al. Nat Med 2018 already established YAP/TAZ → BRD4 → super-enhancers; novelty reduced to incremental (upstream trigger only)
BRD4 phase separation model contested (McSwiggen eLife 2019; Chong Science 2018)
Sabari 2018 showed MED1 phase separation, not BRD4 specifically — misattribution
EP300 kcat ~5/min is unverified in vivo

attacks.novelty: PARTIAL KILL — Zanconato 2018 established YAP→BRD4→super-enhancers. H1 adds ECM stiffness trigger = incremental novelty only.
attacks.mechanism: WOUNDED — plausible chain but BRD4 phase separation threshold in vivo unknown; phase separation model contested
attacks.logic: MINOR — bisociation naming coincidence (supertranscription factor ↔ super-enhancer) not structural
attacks.falsifiability: PASSES — well-designed ChIP-seq across stiffness gradient
attacks.triviality: MODERATE — logical one-hop extension from Zanconato 2018 + Dupont 2011
attacks.counter_evidence: McSwiggen eLife 2019 challenged BRD4 condensate model; 1,6-hexanediol unreliable
attacks.hallucination_check: Low risk — components real but novelty inflated by Zanconato overlap
attacks.claim_verification: No hallucinations; BRD4 phase separation attributed to Sabari 2018 which showed MED1 = misattribution not fabrication

critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Survives because stiffness-gradient super-enhancer ChIP-seq fills a genuine gap (0 papers). Must reframe as testing Zanconato mechanism under physiological stiffness, not claiming new connection.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: LINC-Transmitted Mechanical Force Detaches Enhancers from LADs, Creating Stiffness-Dependent Enhancer Derepression
key_issues: FATAL: Force per lamin-chromatin contact is 0.001-0.01 pN — 100-1000x below detachment threshold
LAD tethering is biochemical (HP1-H3K9me2/3, LBR-lamin), not mechanical — piconewton forces irrelevant
Lamin A/C 'force conductor' argument equally supports opposite conclusion (strengthens LAD tethering)
Sun 2020 showed LAD-region genes RESIST force — direct counter-evidence
Confined migration (>>LINC forces) does not produce LAD reorganization

attacks.novelty: PASSES — no prior work on physical LAD detachment by LINC force
attacks.mechanism: FATAL — force per contact 100-1000x below threshold; biochemical tethering not susceptible to pN forces
attacks.logic: Lamin A/C force conductor = cherry-picking; equally supports LAD strengthening
attacks.counter_evidence: Sun 2020 shows LAD genes resist force; confined migration shows stable LADs under extreme deformation
attacks.hallucination_check: Novel because wrong — no one proposed it because numbers are ~1000x insufficient
attacks.claim_verification: No hallucinations; 'Wang & Bhatt' reference suspiciously vague

critiqued_hypotheses.kill_rationale: Three independent fatal flaws: (1) quantitative force insufficiency, (2) biochemical LAD tethering, (3) direct counter-evidence from Sun 2020 and confined migration

critiqued_hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Coordinates a Dual-Enzyme Bivalent-to-Active Enhancer Switch via Concurrent KDM6B Activation and YAP-EP300 Recruitment
key_issues: UTX (KDM6A), not KDM6B, is the canonical enhancer H3K27me3 demethylase (MLL3/4-COMPASS)
Temporal mismatch: YAP/EP300 (minutes) vs KDM6B upregulation (hours) = sequential not simultaneous
KDM6B targets in Yu 2025 (Snail/Twist promoters) may not overlap YAP-TEAD enhancer targets
GSK-J4 inhibits both KDM6A and KDM6B — cannot distinguish in proposed experiment

attacks.novelty: PASSES — no prior work on coordinated dual-enzyme enhancer switch under ECM stiffness
attacks.mechanism: SIGNIFICANT ISSUES — KDM6B vs UTX at enhancers; temporal mismatch; PRC2 kinetic competition
attacks.logic: MINOR — 'unique to ECM stiffness' overclaimed; other stimuli activate both pathways
attacks.counter_evidence: KDM6B targets (EMT genes) likely different from YAP-TEAD targets (proliferation genes); GSK-J4 off-target effects
attacks.hallucination_check: Low risk — individual enzymes verified; coordination claim parametric but not hallucinated
attacks.claim_verification: No hallucinations; Whitworth 2024 uncertain but not suspicious; Yu 2025 from literature context

critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Strongest hypothesis. Dual-enzyme concept is elegant and genuinely novel. Must address: (1) reformulate with UTX as primary/alternative candidate, (2) reframe as sequential two-phase model, (3) add genetic knockdown (siKDM6B vs siKDM6A).

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Stiffness-Induced Enhancer H3K27ac Creates Self-Sustaining Transcriptional Condensates That Persist as Mechanical Memory
key_issues: H3K27ac half-life ~2-6h precludes 24-72h self-sustaining memory
BRD4 FRAP shows seconds-scale exchange — condensates concentrate but don't trap enzymes
eRNA half-lives ~5-30 minutes — condensate scaffold dissolves quickly after transcription stops
Yang 2014 memory requires >10 days exposure; 48-72h establishment is inconsistent
HDAC activity will erase H3K27ac within hours without sustained YAP-EP300 recruitment

attacks.novelty: PASSES — specific feedback loop mechanism for mechanical memory is novel
attacks.mechanism: SEVERE — H3K27ac turnover and eRNA instability preclude self-sustaining loop; quantitative mismatch with Yang 2014
attacks.logic: MODERATE — assumes positive feedback self-sustains but has no bistability mechanism for 'locking' on-state
attacks.falsifiability: PASSES — excellent time-course design with JQ1 perturbation
attacks.triviality: PASSES — specific molecular mechanism not obvious
attacks.counter_evidence: H3K27ac ~2-6h turnover; eRNA ~5-30min half-life; JQ1 effects within 1-2h contradict days-long condensate stability
attacks.hallucination_check: Components real; self-sustaining claim may be novel because quantitatively impossible at proposed timescale
attacks.claim_verification: Yang 2014 may be Nat Materials not Science — journal uncertain; Henninger 2021 verified but showed RNA broadly not eRNAs specifically

critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Survives because enhancer-level mechanical memory is worth testing. Must revise: (1) memory timescale from 24-72h to 6-12h, (2) feedback loop as short-term bridge not permanent memory, (3) position as first phase of multi-mechanism memory (DNA methylation provides long-term).

critiqued_hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Rewires Enhancer-Promoter 3D Chromatin Loops via YAP-TEAD-Dependent NIPBL Recruitment and Cohesin Redistribution
key_issues: 6-step cascade attenuates signal at each step — may be quantitatively insufficient
CTCF boundaries dominate loop architecture; enhancer-driven cohesin redistribution is minor
BRD4-NIPBL from Olley 2018 may be CdLS-specific, not general
Mechanism trivially follows from known enhancer-cohesin biology
TADs remarkably stable under strong perturbations (Rao 2017 auxin-cohesin experiments)

attacks.novelty: MODERATE — HiChIP data under stiffness would be novel; mechanism is obvious inference
attacks.mechanism: SIGNIFICANT — 6-step cascade; CTCF dominance; BRD4-NIPBL generalizability uncertain
attacks.logic: TRIVIALITY CONCERN — stiffness → active enhancers → cohesin → loops is obvious deduction from premises
attacks.falsifiability: PASSES — HiChIP well-designed with perturbation panel
attacks.triviality: SIGNIFICANT — a grad student knowing Kagey 2010 + Dupont 2011 could predict this
attacks.counter_evidence: Auxin cohesin degradation (Rao 2017) shows genetically determined loop positions; TADs stable under perturbation
attacks.hallucination_check: Low risk — all components real; novelty is in the data not the mechanism
attacks.claim_verification: All citations verified (Kagey 2010, Olley 2018, Fudenberg 2016, Rao 2017, Mumbach 2016)

critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Survives because HiChIP under stiffness is genuinely novel data. Mechanism is trivial extension of known biology. Should be reframed as experimental prediction rather than mechanistic discovery.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Tissue-Specific ECM Stiffness Selects Tissue-Specific Enhancer Programs Through Differential YAP Nuclear Residence Time and EP300 Occupancy Kinetics
key_issues: CONCEPTUAL FLAW: YAP-TEAD targets shared proliferation/survival enhancers, NOT tissue-specific enhancers
Tissue-specific enhancers require lineage TFs (MYOD, RUNX2, PPARγ) that YAP cannot substitute
Morphogen analogy structurally flawed — YAP produces quantitatively graded output of SAME program, not qualitatively different programs
YAP is antagonistic to some tissue programs (inhibits adipogenesis, can inhibit chondrogenesis)
Stiffness is permissive not instructive for differentiation

attacks.novelty: MODERATE — kinetic selection model adds mechanistic layer; graded signal → graded output is general principle
attacks.mechanism: FATAL CONCEPTUAL FLAW — YAP-TEAD targets are not tissue-specific; lineage TFs are required
attacks.logic: SIGNIFICANT — morphogen analogy fails structurally; confuses quantitative grading with qualitative selection
attacks.falsifiability: PASSES but prediction (d) (tissue profile matching) likely false
attacks.triviality: PARTIAL — graded YAP → graded activation at TEAD sites is relatively obvious
attacks.counter_evidence: MSCs on stiff substrate without supplements = minimal differentiation; YAP inhibits adipogenesis
attacks.hallucination_check: Stiffness-as-morphogen is novel because wrong — morphogen model doesn't apply to YAP signaling
attacks.claim_verification: All citations verified (Elosegui-Artola 2017, Engler 2006, Dupont 2011); Levental 2007 journal uncertain

critiqued_hypotheses.survival_note: Survives narrowly if reframed from 'tissue-specific enhancer selection' to 'stiffness-graded YAP-TEAD enhancer activation.' The modest version (hierarchical TEAD enhancer activation at different stiffnesses) is probably correct. Prediction (d) should be flagged as likely false.

critiqued_hypotheses.title: Direct LINC-Transmitted Mechanical Force Functions as a Physical Pioneer Factor, Unwinding Nucleosomes at Mechanosensitive Enhancers
key_issues: FATAL: Per-nucleosome force = 0.00015 pN, which is 13,000x below 2 pN unwrapping threshold
Stress concentration factors (2-50x typical) cannot compensate for 4-order-of-magnitude deficit
Per-nucleosome force is 3000-6000x below thermal noise — signal undetectable above background
Confined migration at >>LINC forces produces nuclear rupture but NOT chromatin opening
Chromatin is not a continuous elastic solid — materials science stress concentration model is category error
Viscoelastic relaxation dissipates forces on sub-second timescales

attacks.novelty: PASSES — physical pioneer factor concept is genuinely creative and novel
attacks.mechanism: FATAL — per-nucleosome force 13,000x below threshold; below thermal noise; wrong force geometry
attacks.logic: SEVERE — materials science stress concentration model is category error for dynamic chromatin polymer
attacks.falsifiability: PASSES with caveats — flavopiridol has pleiotropic effects confounding ATAC-seq
attacks.triviality: PASSES — concept is non-obvious and creative
attacks.counter_evidence: Confined migration (Denais/Raab 2016) at >>LINC forces does not open enhancers; AFM compression causes compaction not opening
attacks.groundedness: 35% — 4/10 claims verifiable; key parametric claims quantitatively refuted
attacks.hallucination_check: Novel because wrong — no one proposed it because numbers are 13,000x insufficient
attacks.claim_verification: Shimamoto 2017 misapplied (mitosis vs interphase); Li 2005 citation uncertain; core force values verified

critiqued_hypotheses.kill_rationale: Overwhelming quantitative impossibility: (1) per-nucleosome force 13,000x below threshold, (2) below thermal noise, (3) wrong force geometry, (4) viscoelastic dissipation, (5) category error in stress concentration model, (6) confined migration counter-evidence, (7) AFM compression counter-evidence. Seven independent lines of evidence converge on impossibility.

critic_questions.question: Zanconato et al. Nat Med 2018 showed YAP/TAZ/TEAD recruit BRD4 to super-enhancers in breast cancer. H1 is mechanistically identical with a different upstream trigger. What is genuinely new? Can you quantitatively argue why stiffness specifically crosses phase separation threshold? Reframe novelty.
BRD4 phase separation model contested (McSwiggen eLife 2019, Chong Science 2018). Can H1 be reformulated WITHOUT phase separation — based on H3K27ac density and BRD4 occupancy alone?

UTX (KDM6A), not KDM6B, is the canonical enhancer H3K27me3 demethylase via MLL3/4-COMPASS. Yu 2025 showed KDM6B at PROMOTERS (Snail, Twist), not enhancers. Reformulate with UTX as primary candidate. What evidence exists for KDM6B at distal enhancers?

YAP (15-60 min) arrives hours before KDM6B (transcriptional upregulation). Reframe as sequential two-phase model: Phase 1 = YAP-EP300 acetylates accessible enhancers; Phase 2 = KDM6B resolves bivalent enhancers. How does sequential operation change predictions?

H3K27ac half-life ~2-6h. BRD4 FRAP = seconds (not trapped). After YAP exits (1-4h on soft ECM), what quantitative argument sustains H3K27ac for 24-72h? Consider revising to 6-12h memory timescale with position as short-term bridge to longer-term DNA methylation memory.

YAP-TEAD targets are TEAD-motif enhancers (CTGF, CYR61) — shared proliferation/survival genes, NOT tissue-specific. Tissue-specific enhancers require MYOD/RUNX2/PPARγ. Revise from 'tissue-specific selection' to 'stiffness-graded TEAD enhancer activation'. Flag prediction (d) as likely false.

5/7 hypotheses use YAP-EP300. The MRTF-SRF pathway (RhoA → actin → MRTF → SRF → CArG-box enhancers) is equally mechanosensitive and targets COMPLETELY DIFFERENT enhancers. Generate hypothesis: does ECM stiffness simultaneously activate two independent enhancer programs (YAP-TEAD + MRTF-SRF)? Do shear stress enhancers (Tsaryk 2022) differ from stiffness enhancers?

meta_critique.kill_rate_assessment: Near lower boundary of healthy range (30-50%). Acceptable for BLIND MODE (no web search to discover additional prior work or counter-evidence).
meta_critique.systematic_bias_detected: 5/7 hypotheses depend on YAP-EP300 as primary mechanism. Pipeline needs MRTF-SRF, PIEZO1-CaMKII, and other non-YAP mechanosensitive pathways.
citation_issues: Yang 2014: journal may be Nat Materials not Science
Sabari 2018: showed MED1 phase separation, hypothesis extrapolates to BRD4 without specific citation
Shimamoto 2017: measured mitotic chromosomes, misapplied to interphase chromatin
meta_critique.overall_quality: Hypotheses are well-structured with honest self-critique. Main weaknesses: (1) YAP-EP300 over-reliance, (2) quantitative insufficiency in force-based hypotheses (H2, H7), (3) overclaiming novelty where prior work exists (H1 vs Zanconato 2018), (4) conceptual errors in tissue-specificity claims (H6).
